item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Seeing as the wiki is currently having large discussions relating to plagiarism and source citation, we (Henzoid and I) thought it would be wise to include the fact that the snippet of quote text in the final addendum is taken from Little Red Riding Hood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I worked out the Wolf thing when it described the doctor's claws, but that image of the Brothers Grimm still got me floored for some reason. Guess I'm just a sucker for non-political historical figures being involved in magical conspiracies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Before I even got to the actual documentation, you already had me kicking myself for never thinking of an "anti-infohazard." I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get that it runs the risk of ruining the punchline, but it seems awfully negligent that the article repeatedly mentions the importance of physically describing the thing, and then never physically describes it. Maybe a line about "see Document XYZ for a list of known appearances."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The stinger really took away from the article, especially the very last textbox about Red Riding Hood. Can't +1 it in good faith.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i really enjoyed this! the reveal was super fun, and i always love alternate history stuff with the foundation :]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope so too! No promises, but preparations for a possible hub may or may not be in the works :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kicked myself for not figuring out what was going on until the end. I was looking for more and when it got to the reveal I realized it was more than enough.
+1 and I'm hoping the Department of Miscommunications gets a hub of its own at some point. I'll just search for them manually until then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OutFUCKINGstanding. Seriously. The lead in paid off so well I got a chill. This is the definition of classic story SCP'ed right. An extremely well earned +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took a while, but then it hit hard. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: love it, can't believe it took this long for a SCP about this kind of subject to be made (unless im missing one)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely superb. Definitely a new favourite for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Welp, I'm proud to say I worked it out before the punchline but this is still a beautiful SCP with a great little trick up its sleeve. +1 wholeheartedly.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So I sort of can't get over the fact that I got to collab with Tanhony on this. Tan is an amazing writer, and you absolutely need to go read all his stuff.

Tanhony's Author Page

Also, I have things too, which are pretty cool

Henzoid's Author Page

Also we got crit from these lovely peeps:

  • MaliceAforethought
  • N. Aepic Fael
  • PsychicProgrammer
  • cluelessduck
  • gee0765

Image: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Brothers_Grimm#/media/File:Grimm.jpg

Anyways, that's about it. Thanks for reading!

EDIT: +100
Wow, thanks you guys! I'm so glad this has become as popular as it has, considering the implications if it doesn't.
